PLAINTIFF THEDA FUNG's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Theda Fung'S Supplemental Interrogatories To Samir F. Daher:

Special Interrogatories-All objections stand from the March 18, 2018 response as the court lost jurisdiction to rule on these objections due to Plaintiff's failure to timely bring a motion to compel further responses. Defendant Daher need only provide information obtained after March 18, 2018. All preliminary and general objections are overruled as there are not allowed by the CCP. All objections on the grounds that the supplementary interrogatory violates CCP 2030.060 are overruled.
#1 Objections on vague, ambiguous and compound are sustained. Furthermore, defendant responded that there were no changes to the response. Defendant need not provide a further response.
#2, 3, 4 Objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further response that answers if he ever had any relationship with the individuals in question.
#5, 6 Objections on the grounds of compound and that the interrogatory contains subparts are sustained. Objection on the grounds of privacy is sustained. Plaintiff has not established that they are entitled to discovery financial information. Defendant need not provide a further response.
#7 Objection on the grounds of subparts is sustained. The sentence "Describe this in detail including all roles YOU played in the transaction" is stricken from the interrogatory. All other objections are overruled.
Defendant to provide a further response.
#8, 9, 10, 12, Objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further response.
#11 Objection on the grounds of subparts is sustained all other objections are overruled. The interrogatory will be broken up as follows: 11(a) "Did YOU act as bookkeeper for Defendants Terra Nova Real Estate Services Inc. and Shideh Rostami?" And 11(b) "If the answer to 11(a) is yes then IDENTIFY all persons with knowledge of your bookkeeping work for these two defendants."
Defendant to serve a further verified supplemental response to Special Interrogatories #2, 3, 4, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12 no later than July 24, 2020.
Form Interrogatories--All objections stand from the March 18, 2018 response as the court lost jurisdiction to rule on these objections due to Plaintiff's failure to timely bring a motion to compel further responses. All objections on the grounds that the supplementary interrogatory violates CCP 2030.060 are overruled.
#15.1. Objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a further response.
#17.1. There is no ruling. The separate statement does not list the language of the request for admission and the response to the request for admission. See CRC, Rule 3.1345(c)(5). Plaintiff to file and serve an amended separate statement no later then July 20, 2020. Defendant may file a response by July 31. 2020. The matter is continued to August 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m.
Defendant to serve a further verified supplemental response to Form Interrogatory #15.1 no later than July 24, 2020.
Requests for sanctions will be heard on August 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m.

Notice Of Motion And Motion: 1. For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioner Served Discovery, 2. For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John S. Rueppel, Attorney at Law, Telephone (415) 992-3123, appearing for Plaintiff Theda Fung;
No other appearance.

No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF THEDA FUNG'S Motion: 1. For Order Compelling Further Responses To Petitioner Served Discovery, 2. For Sanctions

On the motion to compel Defendant Susana Mancia to serve further responses to Form Interrogatories Set No. 1, the Court orders further responses to FI 15.1. Defendant Mancia can properly be required to identify what facts alleged in Plaintiff's complaint she believes are untrue and what facts, witnesses, and documents she relies on for her belief. With regard to FI 12.1, this interrogatory is not tailored to a complaint concerning a series of incidents and the motion to compel further responses is denied.

On the motion to compel Defendant Mancia to serve further responses to Special Interrogatories Set No. 1, the Court grants the motion and orders further responses as to SI 1, SI 2, SI 6, SI 7, and SI 8. The Court denies the motion as to SI 5 and SI 9 because Defendant has responded and Plaintiff's objections are simply that she disagrees with the responses.

On the motion to compel Defendant Mancia to serve further responses to Plaintiff's Demand for Production of Documents Set No. 1, the Court grants the motion. Defendant's opposition papers contend that all responsive documents have been produced but that is not reflected in the response to the demand for production or in the declarations that Defendant filed in opposition to the discovery motion.

On the motion to compel Defendant MGM to serve further responses to Plaintiff's Demand for Production of Documents Set No. 1, Defendants' opposition offers no argument and the Court grants the motion.

The Court denies the parties' respective requests for sanctions. Plaintiff's request is denied because Plaintiff did not submit a supporting declaration showing the time and expense of seeking a discovery order. Defendant's request is denied because Plaintiff's motion does not lack substantial justification.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (506/CVA)
